message:
fix(mvcc): overflow-safe arithmetic, unified truthiness, and actual usable_size

Several correctness issues in the MVCC subsystem are addressed:

Bloom filter (core_types): The second hash function for ReadSetBloom
used rotate_left + XOR which produced poor bit dispersion. Switching
to wrapping_mul with a second prime gives better independent hash
distributions, reducing false-positive rates.

Integer overflow (index_regen): All integer arithmetic (add, sub, mul,
negate, abs) previously used wrapping operations, silently producing
wrong results on overflow. Now uses checked_* with float promotion
on overflow, matching SQLite's behavior where integer overflow falls
back to floating-point representation.

Truthiness evaluation (index_regen): Text/blob values were previously
always treated as falsy. SQLite coerces text to numeric before testing
truthiness — "1" is truthy, "0" is falsy. The NOT operator now
delegates to the unified sqlite_value_is_truthy helper rather than
hand-rolling its own logic.

Shift operations (index_regen): Shifts by >= 64 bits previously used
wrapping_shl/shr which is undefined-adjacent in semantics. Now clamps
to return 0 for shifts >= 64, matching SQLite behavior.

Usable size (physical_merge): Cell parsers hardcoded 4096 as the
usable page size for local-payload computation. This is wrong for
databases with non-default page sizes or reserved bytes. The actual
usable_size is now threaded through from the caller.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
